rulings.Case Title: MIREN R ALVAREZ VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A;, CLEAR RECON CORP;, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 11, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF GRANTED. Plaintiff's complaint filed on July 17, 2024 is dismissed with prejudice as to defendant Bank of America N.A., for failure to amend the complaint following the court's order sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend. (CCP section 581(f)(2); Parsons v. Umansky (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 867, 870.) No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FIRST UNITARIAN UNIVERSALIST SOCIETY VS. MONTESSORI SCHOOL OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 11, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF FIRST UNITARIAN UNIVERSALIST SOCIETY OF SAN FRANCISCO APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. Right to attach order shall issue as to assets held by defendant Montessori School of San Francisco Inc. dba Montessori School of Children only, to secure plaintiff's claim of $284,136.84 allegedly due under the contract. Assets held by non-parties Sunil Wijesinghe and Amala Wijesinghe are exempt from attachment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
